name: Minneapolis & Suburban Bus Co
owner_or_parent: Tiger Infrastructure Partners (via Minnesota Coaches / MNC Transportation platform, Feb 2025)
fleet_size_est: unknown (part of >1,000-vehicle Minnesota Coaches group across 20 terminals)
districts_or_customers: Minneapolis Public Schools and several Twin Cities charter/private schools (as part of Monarch Bus Service / Minnesota Coaches family of companies headquartered in Hastings, MN)
recent_news: Feb 2025: Tiger Infrastructure Partners closed growth investment / acquisition of control of Minnesota Coaches platform (incl. Marschall Line, Rehbein Transit, Ready Bus, Voyageur Bus, Minn-Dakota Coaches, Faribault Transportation). Minneapolis & Suburban Bus operates from the same Hastings HQ (101 10th St E) under the Boaz-family Minnesota Coaches umbrella.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Part of Tiger Infrastructure-backed Minnesota Coaches / MNC Transportation platform (Feb 2025 close) — a competing PE-owned consolidator in the same Upper Midwest school-bus segment.

name: Century Small Bus Solutions
fleet_size_est: likely very small (1-3 vehicles; 3 employees per source data)
districts_or_customers: likely Frederick County Public Schools (Middletown, MD area) — unconfirmed
recent_news: No M&A or news found. Listed website sfs-md.com did not resolve; address (14 W Main St, Middletown, MD 21769) is shared with The Main Cup restaurant, suggesting a mail-drop / non-operational HQ presence.
fit_notes: Too small / opaque to evaluate. Name suggests SPED/small-bus niche which can be valuable for Summit add-on if it holds Frederick County contracts, but no confirmed web presence, fleet data, or principals. Would require direct phone diligence before pursuing.

name: Lake Crystal Bus Svc Inc
owner_or_parent: independent (Heinze family; DBA Lake Crystal Coaches)
fleet_size_est: ~15 motorcoaches (9 power units per USDOT; 15 stated on company site)
districts_or_customers: Historically served Mankato-area schools (school-bus operations 1951-2007); since 1990 primarily luxury motorcoach charter (school athletic trips, theater/bingo, DoD-certified military, business/leisure)
recent_news: No recent M&A. Company exited regular school-bus route service in 2007 and has operated as a charter motorcoach business since.
fit_notes: Weak fit for Summit. Although family-owned and 70+ years old (succession plausible), the business is now a charter motorcoach operator (exited school-bus routes in 2007). Limited recurring school-district contract revenue; primarily activity-trip and adult charter.

name: BJ Bus Svc Inc
employees_est: 1 driver (per USDOT); ~3 employees per source data
service_types: private passenger (non-business) charter; possible school/activity charter
districts_or_customers: Operates in Prince George's County, MD area; no confirmed PGCPS contractor listing
recent_news: No M&A or expansion news. USDOT 805617 ACTIVE with Satisfactory safety rating (Feb 2021 compliance review). Reported revenue ~$119K.
website: Not confirmed (listed website 'Heritagevethospital.com' in source data is a veterinary hospital — mis-association). No company website found.
fit_notes: Too small to be a meaningful tuck-in. Only 2 power units / 1 driver per FMCSA, ~$119K revenue, USDOT-classified 'private passenger (non-business)' rather than authorized school-bus carrier. Likely a 1-owner-operator charter, not a route contractor.

name: Mycareride LLC
districts_or_customers: Central Ohio / Columbus-area school districts; serves McKinney-Vento (homeless/displaced) student population and special-needs students with mobility impairments and developmental disabilities
recent_news: No M&A or material news. Standard small alternative-student-transportation operator.
fit_notes: Niche alternative-student-transportation (ASTC) operator focused on McKinney-Vento + SPED in Columbus. Too small to be a standalone tuck-in but could be relevant if Summit pursues an ASTC/SPED bolt-on theme in Ohio. Founder-led, no clear succession driver.
